title: Pelican to Murder — Level 4 Detective
description: Level 4 version with subtler timeline contradictions and sound booth evidence.

answerKey.method: Tampered with the fog machine and hid Lena’s rescue inhaler during the sound cue
answerKey.motive: To stop Lena from revealing Cole sold the venue’s unreleased event music package
answerKey.keyEvidence: Sound booth automation log, fog-fluid receipt, and inhaler locker photo
answerKey.explanation: Cole’s booth automation log places him at the only console that could trigger the fog cue. The fog-fluid receipt proves preparation, and the locker photo shows he had access to Lena’s inhaler before the sound cue began.

publicClues.title: Case Opened
publicClues.text: Pelican to Murder begins as a live investigation. This session is Level 4: Detective Case.

publicClues.title: Difficulty Loaded
publicClues.text: This is Level 4: Detective Case. Evidence and answers are unique to this level.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 1 Available
publicClues.text: Round 1 is ready. Answer the checkpoint after reviewing the fog cue.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 2 Available
publicClues.text: Round 2 is ready. Compare timeline movement before answering.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 3 Available
publicClues.text: Round 3 is ready. Digital evidence now matters.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available
publicClues.text: Round 4 is ready. Find the motive evidence.

publicClues.title: Final Accusation Open
publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Complete all 10 total questions before the lock.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Lena called Cole twice about “the track package” before the event.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: Cole ignored a call at the exact minute the fog cue was armed.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Lena texted Cole: “You sold the package before approval.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Cole answered: “You don’t understand what that money fixes.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Cole’s wristband remained in the sound booth through the cue window.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Lena’s tracker moved from the host table to the booth-side hallway.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A digital music marketplace payout matches Cole’s account.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The payout label matches the unreleased Pelican to Murder event package.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A photo of the booth shelf shows Lena’s inhaler beside Cole’s headphones.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: A later photo shows the same shelf after the inhaler disappears.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: A guest story shows heavy fog before the scheduled finale.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: Cole reposted the story, then removed it after questions started.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Cole saved a buyer as “PTM package.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: Lena saved the buyer’s number under “unauthorized sale.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: the fog cue was triggered earlier than the script called for.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the inhaler location matters more than the smoke itself.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: Sound automation log shows a manual override from Cole’s console.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: The cue was armed 90 seconds before Lena entered the hallway.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: He also searched “remove watermark from demo music.”

rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. Focus on the fog cue. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the fog cue proves before the next round opens.

rounds.dialogue: Round 3 is open. Focus on the missing inhaler. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close the case.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Pelican to Murder. You are playing Level 4: Detective Case. Study each app carefully. Every level has its own truth path, evidence trail, and final answer set.
revealScript: Case closed. This was Level 4: Detective Case. The culprit was Cole Vinet. The method: Tampered with the fog machine and hid Lena’s rescue inhaler during the sound cue. The motive: To stop Lena from revealing Cole sold the venue’s unreleased event music package. The proof: Sound booth automation log, fog-fluid receipt, and inhaler locker photo.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: Which scene detail is most important?

options.label: A guest laughed during the set
A stool moved beside the bar

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 2: Which clue weakens Cole’s movement story?

options.label: His wristband stayed in the sound booth

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 3: Which browser clue shows preparation?
Checkpoint 4: What gives Cole a motive?

options.label: Unauthorized sale of the music package

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: Which paired clues prove opportunity and method?

options.label: Inhaler photo plus sound automation log

questions.prompt: Final 2: How was the murder carried out?

options.label: Cole triggered the fog cue and hid the inhaler
To hide the unauthorized music package sale
To remove Lena from a guest list

questions.prompt: Final 4: Which evidence best proves it?

options.label: Automation log, fog receipt, and inhaler photo
Cole said the cue ran automatically
Brielle said her live was unedited
